Be recognised.
Save lives.

Identifying, recognising and celebrating hospitals across Asia Pacific that demonstrate excellence in environmental hygiene and patient safety.

About AEHEA

Award programme timeline
Commitment
Innovation
Quality
Evaluation
Recognition
Celebration
01

Commitment to IPC

The APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award recognises hospitals that demonstrate excellence in environmental hygiene and patient safety. The programme is scientifically led and independently governed by APSIC.

Leadership

A/Prof. Moi Lin Ling

Program Chair, AEHEA

The APSIC Environmental Hygiene Excellence Award celebrates healthcare institutions that demonstrate exceptional leadership and measurable success in maintaining safe environments. It is a critical step toward safeguarding patient lives across the region.

"A commitment to patient safety at the highest level."

Prof. Anucha Apisarnthanarak

President, APSIC

One of the core components in the infection prevention and control programme is appropriate environmental hygiene practices and protocols. Together, we can facilitate practices related to the prevention and control of healthcare associated infections.

"This is a huge field for improving patient outcomes."

Our goals

Regional awareness
Recognise excellence
Share best practices
Improve patient safety
Build IPC networks

Recognise excellence across Asia Pacific

By identifying hospitals that demonstrate outstanding commitment to environmental hygiene, we create a regional benchmark for quality patient care. Award recipients gain visibility among peers and inspire continuous improvement in infection prevention practices.

Frequently Asked Questions

Hospitals and healthcare institutions across the Asia Pacific region are eligible to apply. Participation is open to institutions at various stages of environmental hygiene development.
Participation in the Award Programme is generally not associated with a monetary prize or participation fee. Any specific requirements will be communicated during the registration phase.
Submissions will be assessed based on commitment to infection prevention and environmental hygiene practices, innovation and sustainability, and impact on patient safety.
Winners receive formal recognition at APSIC events, exposure across regional networks, and a structured scoring report with expert feedback.
Dates for the upcoming cycle will be published on this website and communicated to registered stakeholders. Subscribe below to receive updates.
Yes. All submissions will be handled with strict confidentiality and used solely for evaluation purposes within the programme.

Want to stay informed?

Get updates on the AEHEA award programme.
